query:
The profiler is set up with Approach A. The app added @nestjs/graphql + @nestjs/apollo + graphql with GraphQLModule.forRoot() (context not customized).

User: Add the GraphQL collector.
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-simple.md. Register GraphQLCollectorModule.forRoot() (the module is GraphQLCollectorModule, NOT ProfilerGraphQLModule) alongside GraphQLModule, gated the same way as the core. Apply the gotcha: the GraphQLModule context must expose the request — for Apollo, context: ({ req }) => ({ req }); without it, GraphQL operations are not profiled.

query: Which collector is ESM-only and what does that imply for wiring it?
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-orm.md. Answer: nest-profiler-mikro-orm is the only ESM-only collector ('type': 'module', .js import specifiers). It must be imported after MikroOrmModule; it wraps the ORM logger on init and needs no dataSource.

query: I installed the HTTP collector but nothing shows up in the HTTP Client panel. Why?
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-http.md. Answer: nothing is captured unless an instrumentation is listed — HttpCollectorModule.forRoot({ instrumentations: [AxiosInstrumentation] }) (from '@eleven-labs/nest-profiler-http/axios'), with HttpModule imported in the SAME module. For fetch use FetchInstrumentation (from '/fetch'); for undici/got inject HttpProfilerRecorder and call .capture(...) or provide a custom HttpInstrumentation.

query: Can I keep my existing global ValidationPipe when I add the validator collector?
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-validator.md. Answer: No — do not register a second global ValidationPipe. The validator collector installs a global APP_PIPE that wraps your pipe; only one global validator at a time. Fold its options into ValidatorCollectorModule.forRoot({ validationPipeOptions: {...} }) and use value imports (not 'import type') for DTOs.

query: The app just added TypeORM. Do I need forRootAsync with inject: [DataSource] to add the collector?
expected_behavior: Must read collectors-orm.md. Answer: No — TypeOrmCollectorModule self-resolves the DataSource via getDataSourceToken(connectionName), so forRoot() is enough (use forRootAsync only to read options like slowThreshold from ConfigService). Place it after TypeOrmModule and match the app's existing gate; optionally add TypeOrmSchemaCollectorModule for the entity panel.
